rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO APARTMENT ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Alternative Writ Of Mandamus, Stay, And Order To Show Casue Re: Ordinance No. 200375
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2020 line 1. Petitioners' Motion For Permptory Writ Of Mandate, Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Petition for Peremptory Writ of Mandate. Hearing Required. =(501/CFH)

By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). rulings.Case Title: BRENDA BESDANSKY VS. STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2020 line 7. Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky is continued to August 14, 2020. The claims arising out of service of an "eviction notice" (e.g. Complaint, paragraph 33) incorporated into every causes of action and expressly additionally stated as the basis for relief in causes of action for retaliation, violation of SFRO 37.9, violation of civil code 1940.2, fraud, and unfair business practices fall within the first prong of the ANTI-SLAPP. The burden is on the Plaintiff to show the probability of prevailing on the merits on the claims arising out of the service of the notice, i.e. present evidence as to all elements of the claim (for example evidence as to each element of fraud claim arising out of allegations contained in paragraphs 33, 107, 152) "based on defendants' wrongful conduct" other than service of the notice as it relates to the paragraphs subject to this motion. By no later than August 7, 2020 Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief in a table format matching the evidence submitted in support of opposition with the elements of each claims arising out of the service of the notice. Format of the table: Column One - Element of a cause of action; Colum Two - citation to evidence, e.g. Declaration of Jane Doe, paragraph 3, lines 2-5. =(501/CFH)

Mori (1957) 153 Cal.App.2d 828; ?De Long v. De Long (1954) 127 Cal.App.2d 373. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA LABITORIA VS. ERLINDA SELMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File And Serve A Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2020 line 9. PLAINTIFF MARIA LABITORIA'S NOTICE OF MOTION For Leave To File And Serve A Supplemental Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File Supplemental Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiff may file a supplemental complain including the new cause of action arising out of breach of an oral settlement agreement. This action will no longer be an unlawful detainer summary proceeding and will proceed as a general civil case. Plaintiff shall have 5 days to file a supplemental complaint. If Plaintiff does not timely file a supplemental complaint, the action will proceed as an unlawful detainer and Plaintiff may bring a motion to enforce the settlement agreement to resolve the existing cause of action for unlawful detainer based on the operative notice in the Complaint. =(501/CFH)
